This series of [[MSE-series|MSE]] droid ran on tracks, making their ability to travel limited. They were able to consume nuts, bolts and other spare parts, including rusted droid parts.<ref name="CGST">''[[Complete Guide to Star Tours]]''</ref><ref name="EE">{{EndorExpress|url=star-wars-adventures/star-tours/2013/05/retro-tokyo-a-look-back-at-tdls-star-tours-before-it-was-updated|text=Retro-Tokyo! A look back at TDL's Star Tours before it was updated...}}</ref> This model was one of the earliest of the MSE series.<ref name="Ultimate AFC">''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Action Figure Collection]]''</ref>

[[Star Tours/Legends|Star Tours]], a short-lived travel business, had two of these droids.<ref name="ST" /> One was named [[MSE-1T]],<ref name="Hasbro" /> while the other unfortunate unit was damaged and found a new use as a table for [[G3-5LE]]. The droid models frequented the [[Droidnostics Center]] of the spaceport.<ref name="CGST" />

==Behind the scenes==
In 2005, [[Hasbro]] released MSE-1T as an exclusive [[Star Tours (real-world)|''Star Tours'']] action figure, but its model name has not been revealed.

The droids are not exclusively found in the [[Disney's Hollywood Studios]] version of Star Tours, but also at [[Tokyo Disneyland]]. According to the "Spot the Droid" poster for the Tokyo starport, they made their homes in the Droidnostics center, and in a map of the ride, also noted that passengers are often extremely lucky to find one.
